title: The Evolution of Desire
subtitle: Strategies of Human Mating
author: David M. Buss
themes: Human mating follows evolved strategies shaped by natural and sexual selection
Men and women face different adaptive problems in mating, leading to different evolved strategies
Mate preferences are not arbitrary — they reflect cues to reproductive value and investment potential
Jealousy, love, commitment, and conflict all have evolutionary logic
Mating strategies are context-dependent — short-term and long-term strategies differ
Understanding evolved mating psychology illuminates modern relationship dynamics
excerpt: Human mating is a rich and complex phenomenon — a source of our deepest joys and our most profound sorrows. But beneath the cultural diversity and individual variation, there are universal patterns of desire. These patterns are not arbitrary. They are the product of millions of years of evolution, shaped by the adaptive problems our ancestors faced in the quest to reproduce.
